item.title: Advancing drug discovery for Inflammatory bowel diseases through human intestinal organoid-based models.
item.description: Expert opinion on drug discovery - Review - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD), including Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis, are chronic inflammatory conditions that affect millions of patients worldwide. Despite recent advances, the ava...

item.title: Short-term Surgical Outcomes After Transanal Restorative Proctocolectomy With Ileal Pouch-Anal Anastomosis: A Comparison of Close Rectal Dissection And Modified Total Mesorectal Excision.
item.description: Diseases of the colon and rectum - The preferred treatment for patients with medically refractory ulcerative colitis is a restorative proctocolectomy with ileal pouch anal anastomosis. The transanal approach has been adopted in many...

item.title: Cost-effectiveness of treatment and care of patients with gastrointestinal inflammatory diseases: a systematic review.
item.description: Journal of medical economics - Systematic review - The aim of this study was to systematically review and descriptively synthesize published cost-effectiveness evidence for biologic versus non-biologic treatments as well as comparisons among differ...

item.title: [Obesity meets Inflammatory Bowel Disease - a Double Whammy? A narrative Review].
item.description: Zeitschrift fur Gastroenterologie - Review - Obesity is increasingly recognised as an important factor influencing disease course and treatment outcomes in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Despite growing evidence that obesity is associated ...

item.title: Disease reprogression following surgical resection in Crohn disease.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Ileocecal resection (ICR) for stricturing (B2) or internal penetrating (B3) phenotypes of Crohn disease (CD) is associated with higher rate of recurrence. Whether patients are at an increased risk ...

item.title: Anatomy is not activity: rethinking MRI fistula volume in perianal Crohn's disease.

item.title: Identification of a druggable target that predicts postoperative Crohn's disease recurrence.
item.description: Gastroenterology - Molecular phenotyping of Crohn's disease (CD) trajectories after ileocolonic resection (ICR) enables the identification of evolving disease mechanisms and related biomarker discovery. Here, we aime...

item.title: Interpreting a Negative Trial in Complex Perianal Crohn's Disease: The End of Allogenic stem cell treatment for fistula patients?

item.title: Balloon dilation versus endoscopic stricturotomy in the treatment of Crohn's anastomotic stricture: experimental randomized study.
item.description: Surgical endoscopy - Up to 80% of patients with Crohn's disease (CD) require surgery, and anastomotic strictures are a frequent postoperative complication with limited response to medical therapy. Endoscopic balloon di...

item.title: Transabdominal intestinal ultrasound versus transmural histopathological findings in severe ulcerative colitis requiring colectomy.
item.description: Crohn's & colitis 360 - Transabdominal intestinal ultrasound (IUS) is a promising, non-invasive tool for monitoring ulcerative colitis (UC). This modality has the advantage of assessing intestinal inflammation transmurall...

item.title: Current and emerging approaches to manage chronic inflammatory gut disorders.
item.description: Frontiers in cellular and infection microbiology - Review - Chronic inflammatory gastrointestinal disorders, including inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), remain challenging to manage du...

item.title: Analysis of the current status of research on perianal lesions associated with Crohn's disease: a bibliometric and visualization analysis.
item.description: Frontiers in surgery - To analyze the characteristics and research hotspots of perianal lesions associated with Crohn's disease (CD) based on the currently published literature. We conducted a comprehensive search of all...

item.title: Targeted nutritional prehabilitation for high-risk Crohn's disease patients undergoing elective gastrointestinal surgery: a case series.
item.description: Frontiers in nutrition - Case report - Crohn's disease (CD) is a chronic inflammatory bowel condition often leading to complications requiring surgery. Optimizing nutrition before surgery contributes to reducing postoperative complicati...
